title: What if Gendo Was More Like Genji
display_title: What if Gendo Was More Like Genji
wikitext:
[]: What if Gendo Was More Like Genji?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:07 pm From Murasaki Shikibu's famous novel about an emperor who becomes a commoner and starts a career as an imperial officer...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:10 pm et tu mi Jimbo? Xard wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: In B4 Permanent termination of "What if X was like Y?" threads.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:11 pm Just because these threads are destined to die an awkward but awesomely bloody death doesn't mean I don't want to go down fighting too.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:14 pm *plays Air on G string in memoriam* I see what you mean... Correction: as emperor he obviously wouldn't have sword... (it would help if I'd ever read Genji Monogatari...)

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:19 pm Anyway, I think he'd be more formal. And he'd have sword and shit

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:22 pm Defectron wrote: Anyway, I think he'd be more formal. And he'd have sword and shit

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:23 pm Xard wrote: (it would help if I'd ever read Genji Monogatari...)

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:24 pm Gendo's language is very unpolite, pretty much lacking all the honoriffics etc. He also never wears his uniform properly. And he certainly isn't gentleman or man of good manners in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:46 pm Defectron wrote: Gendo's language is very unpolite, pretty much lacking all the honoriffics etc. He also never wears his uniform properly. And he certainly isn't gentleman or man of good manners in general.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Tue Dec 29, 2009 7:49 pm I would pay to see a chess game between Picard & Commander Ikari

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Tue Dec 29, 2009 10:10 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: From Murasaki Shikibu's famous novel about an emperor who becomes a commoner and starts a career as an imperial officer...

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Wed Dec 30, 2009 12:26 am Defectron wrote: Gendo's language is very unpolite, pretty much lacking all the honoriffics etc. He also never wears his uniform properly. And he certainly isn't gentleman or man of good manners in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Wed Dec 30, 2009 1:08 am AVman9 wrote: I will say Fuyutsuki wins for formality.
